entries.title: Class 1-A Chooses To Bring Deku Back

context.label: Class 1-A intervenes to bring Deku back

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Class 1-A acts together to retrieve Deku because leaving him alone would destroy him.
content.injection: Class 1-A should approach Deku as friends staging an intervention, not as enemies trying to defeat him.

content.fact: The class uses coordinated movement, Quirks, and emotional knowledge to slow Deku down.
content.injection: Make the retrieval a teamwork sequence: individual appeals matter, but the class succeeds together.

content.fact: Bakugo recognizes the dangerous pattern behind Deku’s self-sacrificing isolation.
content.injection: Bakugo should cut through Deku’s excuses sharply because he understands Deku’s worst heroic habit.

entries.title: Iida Reaches Deku By Taking His Hand

context.label: Iida reaches Deku through Class 1-A teamwork

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Iida physically reaches Deku at the climax of the retrieval, answering Deku’s attempt to outrun help.
content.injection: Use Iida’s reach as friendship and reciprocity: Deku once saved him, and now Iida refuses to let him run alone.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Bakugo apologizes to Deku for years of mistreatment and uses Izuku’s given name in a vulnerable moment.
content.injection: Bakugo’s apology should be sincere and difficult; it does not erase the past, but it changes the emotional baseline.

entries.title: Todoroki Frames Help As Shared Burden

context.label: Class 1-A intervenes to bring Deku back

content.fact: Todoroki emphasizes that friends can share responsibility instead of letting Deku carry everything alone.
content.injection: Todoroki’s appeal should connect to shared burdens, not simple orders to come home.

entries.title: Class 1-A Fights To Save Not Defeat

context.label: Class 1-A intervenes to bring Deku back

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The class battle against Deku is a rescue attempt, not a contest of superiority.
content.injection: Do not write classmates as trying to humiliate or overpower Deku for victory; their objective is to stop his collapse.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

content.fact: Deku is finally too exhausted and emotionally overwhelmed to keep rejecting everyone.
content.injection: Let collapse come from accumulated fatigue and emotional pressure, not one simple argument instantly changing his mind.

entries.title: U.A. Barrier Is A Fortress System

context.label: Civilians at U.A. reject Deku sheltering there

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: U.A. has a large-scale defensive barrier and evacuation system designed for the postwar emergency.
content.injection: Treat U.A. as hardened infrastructure during this period; it is not merely a school building with locked doors.

entries.title: Ochaco Defends Deku Right To Rest

context.label: Ochaco speaks for Deku before civilians

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Ochaco argues publicly that Deku is a person who needs shelter, not just a weapon or threat vector.
content.injection: Ochaco’s speech should rehumanize Deku before frightened civilians; the point is rest and shared responsibility.

entries.title: Kota And Fox Woman Mark Civilian Trust

context.label: Ochaco speaks for Deku before civilians

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Kota and the rescued fox-like woman help show that civilians can recognize Deku’s humanity amid fear.
content.injection: Use small civilian gestures to soften the crowd after Ochaco’s speech; trust returns through people, not slogans.

entries.title: Deku Returns To Rest Not Victory

context.label: Deku is allowed to rest at U.A.

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Deku’s return to U.A. is a pause for recovery, not the end of the crisis.
content.injection: After Deku returns, let him rest and reconnect while the threat of AFO remains unresolved.

entries.title: All Might And Stain Reframe Hero Ideals

context.label: Stain confronts All Might after the collapse

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Stain confronts All Might with evidence that his symbol still survives through ordinary people inspired by him.
content.injection: The Stain meeting should be ideological and somber; it restores perspective, not All Might’s lost power.

content.fact: Stain’s respect for All Might does not make him safe, gentle, or broadly pro-hero.
content.injection: Do not soften Stain into a normal ally; his ideology remains violent and selective.

entries.title: Civilian Fear Is Understandable But Volatile

context.label: Civilians at U.A. reject Deku sheltering there

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Civilians have rational reasons to fear Deku’s presence, but collective fear can become unjust and dangerous.
content.injection: Write U.A. crowd tension as morally complicated: fear is real, but dehumanizing Deku is the failure.

entries.title: Deku Needs Community To Remain A Hero

context.label: Deku is allowed to rest at U.A.

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The arc establishes that Deku’s heroism collapses without rest, community, and mutual protection.
content.injection: Use post-return scenes to reinforce mutual support; solo martyrdom should no longer be treated as the correct answer.

entries.title: U.A. Refuge Does Not Mean Public Trust Is Fixed

context.label: Deku is allowed to rest at U.A.

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Allowing Deku inside U.A. does not instantly repair national trust in heroes.
content.injection: Keep the wider public crisis active after Ochaco’s speech; one crowd softening is not nationwide restoration.
